member.office_address: 2267 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3208

form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Community Project Funding Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Thank you for your interest in Leader Jeffries' FY 2027 Community Project Funding process. This form is meant to collect more information on your proposed project and its eligibility to meet the appropriations committee criteria. The deadline for community project funding requests is COB Monday March 9, 2026.
The House Appropriations committee has released the Fiscal Year 2027 guidelines for requesting community project funding. Consistent with prior years, projects will be restricted to a limited number of federal funding streams and only a handful of projects from each Representative may actually be funded. Only state and local governments and eligible non-profit entities on a very limited basis are permitted to receive funding. The FY2027 guidance and further information on eligible funding streams is available HERE 
Organizations that would like to apply for FY2027 funding may use this form to provide formation about their proposed projects so that our team may begin reviewing applications. Applicants may be asked to provide further information throughout the vetting process.
Should you have any additional questions or concerns please do not hesitate to contact us at NY08Funding@mail.house.gov or 202-225-5936.

sections.description: Basic details regarding the project scope and requested funding amounts.

fields.label: Project Name: A short name by which the project may be identified, including a very brief description of what the funds will be used for and the project's location (city, county, State, Congressional District, or Tribe).  This description may be used to list the project in the House report and should be as accurate as possible to ensure that the funding goes to the correct project and location.
fields.help_text: Enter a concise name and location for House report listing.
fields.draft_value:
* Science Mission Directorate (SMD) - Programmatic Funding
* Funding for robotic exploration and scientific research across Earth science, planetary science, astrophysics, and heliophysics
* National scope with significant activity in Brooklyn, NY (NY-08) via institutions like Honeybee Robotics and CUNY
fields.rationale: Identifies the specific NASA directorate and references local NY-08 recipients (Honeybee Robotics and CUNY) as requested in the field label.

fields.label: Is the funding requested by a governmental or non-profit organization?
Amount requested for FY27
fields.help_text: The dollar amount requested for the current fiscal year cycle.
fields.rationale: This is the specific programmatic funding ask amount for NASA SMD provided in the context.

fields.label: Total project cost
fields.help_text: The full budget for the project from all sources.
fields.rationale: This value reflects the total programmatic funding request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: General description of the project, what funding will be used for and what issues it is addressing (2000 character limit)
fields.draft_value:
* Funds NASA SMD to execute National Academies Decadal Survey priorities in planetary science, astrophysics, heliophysics, and earth science
* Addresses critical workforce stability needs following FY2026 disruptions that impacted thousands of contractors and civil servants
* Sustains U.S. competitive advantage in space science against rapid expansion by international rivals like China
* Provides steady demand for cutting-edge R&D and high-tech manufacturing within the domestic aerospace industrial base
fields.rationale: Combines programmatic goals with strategic talking points regarding competition and workforce stability.

fields.label: Amount of funding enacted in FY26 (if applicable)
fields.help_text: Provide the enacted dollar amount from the previous fiscal year.
fields.rationale: This is the authoritative FY2026 enacted amount for NASA Science (H.R. 6938) provided in the context.

fields.label: Can the project obligate all appropriated funds within 12 months after enactment?
fields.rationale: NASA Science Mission Directorate accounts are structured to obligate programmatic funds within the fiscal year to maintain mission schedules and grant cycles.

fields.label: Please include details on the expected itemized expenses that this CPF funding would be allocated for:
fields.draft_value:
* Research and Analysis (R&A) grants to universities and research institutions
* Mission development, flight hardware procurement, and spacecraft assembly
* Mission operations and data management for active spacecraft fleets
* Technology development for future planetary defense and exploration capabilities
fields.rationale: Lists standard programmatic expenditure categories for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Has your organization received funding from any NYS or NYC agency's in the past? If so please describe the nature of the funding and the amount.
fields.help_text: Describe prior funding including dollar figures.
fields.rationale: This field requires specific internal funding history from the submitter's organization which is not available in the provided context.

sections.description: Contact information and geographic locations for the recipient and the project.

fields.label: Recipient point of contact
Recipient phone number and email address
Recipient legal name and address
Project address
In which congressional district(s) is the recipient located?
In which congressional districts will the funding be used?
fields.rationale: NASA Science funding impacts all 50 states and over 75% of congressional districts. Specifically, NY-08 has received $1,546,341.23 in recent years, supporting institutions like the Research Foundation of the City University of New York.

sections.description: Information on project priority, community support, and regulatory compliance.

fields.label: Request explanation: Explanation of the request must include purpose and why it is a valuable use of taxpayer funds (2000 character limit)
fields.draft_value:
* Advancing science, security, and economic competitiveness as a fundamental public responsibility under Title 51 of the U.S. Code
* Strong economic ROI: every $1 invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the economy
* Sustains the premier global aerospace talent pipeline, inspiring the next generation of STEM professionals and entrepreneurs
* Protects U.S. leadership and prevents the loss of hard-won technical capabilities built over decades
fields.rationale: Uses strategic advocacy talking points to justify the use of taxpayer funds through economic and competitive lenses.

fields.label: What are the benefits of this project to NY08 and why is it a priority?
fields.draft_value:
* Supports high-tech employment and research at NY-08 institutions including Honeybee Robotics and CUNY Brooklyn
* Direct local impact of $1,546,341 in NASA Science obligations within the district (FY2023-FY2025)
* Enables specific research at the Research Foundation of CUNY, including meteorite analysis and lunar geology studies
* Enhances STEM opportunities for local community college students through astronomical observation research programs
fields.rationale: Cites specific NASA spending data and local grant recipients (Honeybee Robotics and Research Foundation of CUNY) located in NY-08.

fields.label: What is the estimated start/completion date of the project?
fields.rationale: This represents the start of Fiscal Year 2027, the period for which this programmatic funding is being requested.

fields.label: Who are the community partners participating in this project?  Have local community development organizations with prior experience with HUD programs been consulted?
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request for NASA Science; the HUD-specific community partner requirements for EDI projects are not applicable.

fields.label: EDI projects, like all projects funded by HUD, are subject to requirements under the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA), HUD's NEPA-implementing regulations at 24 CFR Part 50 or 24 CFR Part 58, and all applicable federal environmental and historic preservation laws, regulations, and Executive Orders. An environmental review must be completed before HUD funds and new commitments of non-HUD funds can be used on a project (24 CFR 58.22) . Does the project require an environmental review? If so, what is the status and/or outcome of the environmental review and National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) category of action (if applicable)?
fields.rationale: This field applies to HUD-funded EDI projects; as a programmatic NASA request, these specific regulations do not apply in this context.

fields.label: Please indicate entities or local leaders that would be willing to provide a letter of support:
fields.draft_value: The Planetary Society, American Astronomical Society (AAS), American Geophysical Union (AGU), and the Research Foundation of the City University of New York.
fields.rationale: These organizations are either allied advocacy groups or active NASA grant recipients within NY-08, such as the Research Foundation of CUNY which has received over $1.3M in SMD funding.

top_grants.recipient_name: Research Foundation of the City University of New York
top_grants.description: Petrologic  geochemical and isotopic diversity among enstatite chondrites
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing the chemical makeup of primitive stony meteorites.

top_grants.recipient_name: Research Foundation of the City University of New York
top_grants.description: Alteration of the surfaces of the moon and other airless bodies due to interactions with the space environment is broadly known as space weathering and encompasses effects from both irradiation by the solar wind and micrometeorite impacts. However  shock and other effects from large impacts can also alter surface material  and specific contributions of either impact or irradiation are not well established nor quantified. In order to disentangle effects of impact from irradiation induced damage  we propose a series of shock experiments on lunar-like anorthite followed by infrared reflectance spectroscopy and Transmission Electron Microscopy of shock recovery experiments. Specifically  we have the following objectives: a) characterize the shock effects in plagioclase of lunar composition in the near infrared (NIR) and mid infrared (MIR) spectral range b) determine the mechanism of deformation and alteration through coordinated petrographic  spectroscopic  and Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) analyses c) determine the effect of particle size and porosity on the production of and remote detection of shock effects in lunar-relevant plagioclase through comparison of experimentally shocked rocks and a fine particulate regolith analog to accomplish these goals we will conduct the following tasks task 1: shock recovery experiments at the johnson space center Flat Plate Accelerator (FPA) and petrographic analysis of recovered materials at the american museum of natural history task 2: nir and mir spectroscopy of pre- and post-shocked material at university of oxford task 3: Transmission Electron Microscopy at the naval research lab the proposed work is relevant to the solar systems workings program   which supports research into atmospheric  climatological  dynamical  geologic  geophysical and geochemical processes occurring on planetary bodies.  Impact cratering and shock metamorphism are dominant geologic processes that we are investigating with application to the moon. The results from our investigation will also enable better interpretations of observations of Mercury and other airless bodies with crusts that include feldspar-rich materials. This work also falls within the subcategory of evolution and modification of surfaces (c-3 of the solar system workings solicitation)  which specifically denotes impact craters and related processes.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Separating impact damage from solar weathering on lunar geology.

top_grants.recipient_name: Research Foundation of the City University of New York
top_grants.description: Kingsborough community college is here to observe
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting community college astronomical observation research over five years.
